arguments.file_text:
# Javadoc Documentation Summary
## Kafka Record Batch Serialization Classes
### `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/`

---

## Overview

This document summarises the Javadoc added to three classes that form the core of Kafka's
record-batch serialization layer. No implementation logic was changed; only documentation was
added or enhanced.

| File | Lines before | Lines after | Javadoc blocks added/enhanced |
|------|-------------|-------------|-------------------------------|
| `RecordBatch.java` | ~270 | 449 | 31 |
| `DefaultRecordBatch.java` | ~720 | 1 009 | 15 |
| `MemoryRecords.java` | ~835 | 1 322 | 39 |

---

## 1. `RecordBatch.java`

**Role:** Top-level interface for a single record batch. Extends `Iterable<Record>`.

### Class-level Javadoc added

- Magic version history (v0 → v1 → v2) and what each version introduced.
- Wire-format overview of the v2 batch header (61-byte `RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD`):
  `BaseOffset(8) | Length(4) | PartitionLeaderEpoch(4) | Magic(1) | CRC(4) | Attributes(2) |
  LastOffsetDelta(4) | BaseTimestamp(8) | MaxTimestamp(8) | ProducerId(8) | ProducerEpoch(2) |
  BaseSequence(4) | RecordsCount(4) | Records[…]`
- CRC-32C coverage: from `Attributes` to end of batch (fields before `Attributes` are excluded).
- Attributes bit layout: bits 2–0 = compression, bit 3 = timestamp type, bit 4 = transactional,
  bit 5 = control, bit 6 = delete horizon, bits 7–15 = unused.
- Compaction invariants: empty batches (zero records) are preserved to maintain producer sequence
  continuity; the `deleteHorizonMs` field repurposes `BaseTimestamp` when the delete-horizon flag
  is set.
- Thread-safety guarantee: **not thread-safe**; external synchronisation required for concurrent
  access.
- Performance characteristics: header field accessors are O(1) buffer reads; `iterator()` may
  trigger decompression.

### Interface constants documented

| Constant | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `MAGIC_VALUE_V0` | Legacy message format (no timestamps) |
| `MAGIC_VALUE_V1` | Timestamp support added |
| `MAGIC_VALUE_V2` | Current format: variable-length records, CRC-32C, producer IDs |
| `CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE` | Alias for `MAGIC_VALUE_V2` |
| `NO_TIMESTAMP` | Sentinel: timestamp not set (`-1`) |
| `NO_SEQUENCE` | Sentinel: sequence not set (`Integer.MIN_VALUE`) |
| `NO_PRODUCER_ID` | Sentinel: producer ID not set (`-1`) |
| `NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH` | Sentinel: producer epoch not set (`-1`) |
| `NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH` | Sentinel: leader epoch not set (`-1`) |
| `RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD` | Fixed header size in bytes (`61`) |

### Methods documented (selected)

| Method | Key documentation points |
|--------|--------------------------|
| `baseOffset()` | First offset in the batch; set by broker on append |
| `lastOffset()` | `baseOffset + lastOffsetDelta`; last offset in the batch |
| `nextOffset()` | `lastOffset + 1`; first offset of the next batch |
| `magic()` | Format version byte |
| `checksum()` | CRC-32C as unsigned 32-bit integer |
| `isValid()` | Returns `false` rather than throwing on CRC mismatch |
| `ensureValid()` | Throws `InvalidRecordException` on CRC mismatch |
| `maxTimestamp()` | Max CREATE_TIME or broker LOG_APPEND_TIME |
| `offsetOfMaxTimestamp()` | Offset of the record with `maxTimestamp()` |
| `timestampType()` | `CREATE_TIME` or `LOG_APPEND_TIME` |
| `producerId()` | Idempotent/transactional producer ID |
| `producerEpoch()` | Producer epoch for fencing |
| `baseSequence()` / `lastSequence()` | Sequence range; wrap-around arithmetic |
| `hasProducerId()` | `true` when `producerId != NO_PRODUCER_ID` |
| `isTransactional()` | Transactional flag from attributes bit 4 |
| `isControlBatch()` | Control flag from attributes bit 5 |
| `deleteHorizonMs()` | Delete horizon from `BaseTimestamp` when bit 6 is set |
| `partitionLeaderEpoch()` | Leader epoch stamped by broker |
| `countOrNull()` | Record count or `null` for v0/v1 batches |
| `isCompressed()` | `true` when compression type ≠ NONE |
| `compressionType()` | Compression algorithm from attributes bits 2–0 |
| `sizeInBytes()` | Total batch size including header |
| `writeTo(WritableChannel)` | Writes all bytes to a channel |
| `streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)` | Lazy, decompression-aware iterator |
| `iterator()` | Materialises all records; prefer `streamingIterator` in hot paths |

---

## 2. `DefaultRecordBatch.java`

**Role:** Concrete implementation of `MutableRecordBatch` (extends `AbstractRecordBatch`).
Wraps a `ByteBuffer` and provides both read and in-place-write access to a v2 batch.

### Class-level Javadoc added

- Wire-format schema with byte offsets for every field in the 61-byte header.
- CRC-32C computation: covers bytes from `Attributes` (offset 17 from batch start) to end of
  batch; fields `BaseOffset`, `Length`, `PartitionLeaderEpoch`, `Magic`, and `CRC` itself are
  excluded.
- Attributes bit layout (same as `RecordBatch` interface, documented with concrete bit positions).
- Timestamp semantics: `BaseTimestamp` holds the first record's timestamp normally; when the
  delete-horizon flag (bit 6) is set, `BaseTimestamp` is repurposed to hold the delete horizon
  in milliseconds.
- Compaction invariants: empty batches are preserved with `RecordsCount = 0` to maintain
  idempotent producer sequence state.
- Thread-safety: **not thread-safe**; the backing `ByteBuffer` is mutated by `setLastOffset`,
  `setMaxTimestamp`, `setPartitionLeaderEpoch`, and `writeHeader`.
- Performance: all header field reads are O(1) `ByteBuffer.get*` calls; `iterator()` on
  compressed batches triggers decompression into a temporary buffer.

### Methods documented

| Method | Key documentation points |
|--------|--------------------------|
| `baseTimestamp()` | First record timestamp; repurposed as delete horizon when bit 6 set |
| `recordInputStream(BufferSupplier)` | Returns decompressed `InputStream` over record data |
| `iterator()` | Delegates to compressed or uncompressed iterator |
| `skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier)` | Lightweight iterator that skips key/value bytes |
| `setLastOffset(long)` | Back-calculates base offset; does **not** recompute CRC |
| `setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType, long)` | Updates attributes + max timestamp; recomputes CRC if changed |
| `setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int)` | Writes epoch; no CRC recomputation needed |
| `writeEmptyHeader(…)` | Writes a zero-record batch header for compaction tombstones |
| `writeHeader(…)` | Full 17-parameter static factory; computes and embeds CRC-32C |
| `sizeInBytes(long, Iterable<Record>)` | Exact uncompressed size for a set of `Record`s |
| `sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord>)` | Exact uncompressed size for a set of `SimpleRecord`s |
| `incrementSequence(int, int)` | Modular increment wrapping at `Integer.MAX_VALUE` |
| `decrementSequence(int, int)` | Modular decrement wrapping below 0 |

---

## 3. `MemoryRecords.java`

**Role:** `Records` implementation backed by a `ByteBuffer`. Primary in-memory representation
of Kafka log data for both producers and consumers. Extends `AbstractRecords`.

### Class-level Javadoc added

- Two construction paths: `readableRecords(ByteBuffer)` (zero-copy wrap) and `builder(…)` +
  `MemoryRecordsBuilder.build()` (new batch construction).
- Filtering and compaction: `filterTo()` applies a `RecordFilter` at both batch and record level,
  re-encodes surviving records, and returns a `FilterResult` with metrics.
- Slicing: `slice(int, int)` returns a zero-copy sub-range view.
- Thread-safety: **not thread-safe**; `validBytes()` is lazily computed and cached (safe to call
  from multiple threads only if the result is idempotent, which it is, but no volatile guarantee).
- Performance: `sizeInBytes()` O(1); `validBytes()` O(n) first call, O(1) thereafter; batch
  iteration lazy and zero-copy; record iteration may trigger decompression.

### Methods documented

#### Instance methods

| Method | Key documentation points |
|--------|--------------------------|
| `sizeInBytes()` | O(1); returns `buffer.limit()` |
| `writeTo(TransferableChannel, int, int)` | Non-blocking partial write; returns bytes written |
| `writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel)` | Blocking loop until all bytes written |
| `validBytes()` | Lazy O(n) scan; cached after first call |
| `firstBatchSize()` | Peeks at first batch header; returns `null` if buffer too short |
| `filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)` | Core compaction/retention operation |
| `buffer()` | Returns a duplicate (shared bytes, independent position/limit) |
| `slice(int, int)` | Zero-copy sub-range view; truncated silently if size exceeds limit |
| `batches()` | Lazy `Iterable<MutableRecordBatch>` over all batches |

#### Static factory methods

| Method | Key documentation points |
|--------|--------------------------|
| `readableRecords(ByteBuffer)` | Zero-copy wrap; buffer not copied |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)` | Current magic, no producer metadata |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long, int)` | Adds `maxSize` cap |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)` | Explicit magic |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long)` | Explicit magic + log append time |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, int)` | Adds partition leader epoch |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, long, long, short, int, boolean)` | Idempotent/transactional producer metadata |
| `withRecords(…)` (6 overloads) | Build + seal; vary magic, offset, compression, epoch |
| `withIdempotentRecords(…)` (3 overloads) | Build + seal with producer ID/epoch/sequence |
| `withTransactionalRecords(…)` (3 overloads) | Build + seal with transactional flag |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(…)` (3 overloads) | Commit/abort control batch |
| `writeEndTransactionalMarker(…)` | Writes end-transaction batch into caller-supplied buffer |
| `withLeaderChangeMessage(…)` | KRaft leader change control batch |
| `withSnapshotHeaderRecord(…)` | KRaft snapshot header control batch |
| `withSnapshotFooterRecord(…)` | KRaft snapshot footer control batch |
| `withVotersRecord(…)` | KRaft voters control batch |
| `withKRaftVersionRecord(…)` | KRaft version control batch |

---

## Cross-References

```
RecordBatch (interface)
  └── AbstractRecordBatch (abstract)
        └── DefaultRecordBatch (concrete, MutableRecordBatch)

Records (interface)
  └── AbstractRecords (abstract)
        └── MemoryRecords (concrete)

MemoryRecords uses DefaultRecordBatch internally (via ByteBufferLogInputStream)
MemoryRecordsBuilder produces MemoryRecords and writes DefaultRecordBatch headers
```

Key `@see` cross-references embedded in the Javadoc:

| From | To |
|------|----|
| `RecordBatch.streamingIterator` | `RecordBatch.iterator` |
| `RecordBatch.isValid` | `RecordBatch.ensureValid` |
| `RecordBatch.deleteHorizonMs` | `RecordBatch.isControlBatch`, `RecordBatch.isTransactional` |
| `DefaultRecordBatch.setLastOffset` | `setMaxTimestamp`, `setPartitionLeaderEpoch` |
| `DefaultRecordBatch.writeHeader` | `writeEmptyHeader` |
| `DefaultRecordBatch.incrementSequence` | `decrementSequence` |
| `DefaultRecordBatch.sizeInBytes(long,Iterable)` | `sizeInBytes(Iterable)` |
| `MemoryRecords.writeTo` | `writeFullyTo` |
| `MemoryRecords.validBytes` | `sizeInBytes`, `firstBatchSize` |
| `MemoryRecords.filterTo` | `RecordFilter`, `FilterResult` |
| `MemoryRecords.slice` | `buffer` |
| `MemoryRecords.readableRecords` | `builder(…)` |
| `MemoryRecords.withEndTransactionMarker` | `writeEndTransactionalMarker` |

---

## Thread-Safety Summary

| Class | Thread-safe? | Notes |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| `RecordBatch` (interface) | N/A | Contract only |
| `DefaultRecordBatch` | **No** | `setLastOffset`, `setMaxTimestamp`, `setPartitionLeaderEpoch` mutate the backing buffer |
| `MemoryRecords` | **No** | `validBytes` cache is non-volatile; `filterTo` mutates destination buffer |

---

## Performance Characteristics Summary

| Operation | Complexity | Notes |
|-----------|-----------|-------|
| Header field read (any) | O(1) | Direct `ByteBuffer.get*` at fixed offset |
| `RecordBatch.isValid()` | O(n) | CRC computed over entire batch payload |
| `MemoryRecords.sizeInBytes()` | O(1) | `buffer.limit()` |
| `MemoryRecords.validBytes()` | O(n) first call, O(1) after | Iterates all batches once, then caches |
| `MemoryRecords.batches()` | O(1) to obtain iterator, O(1) per batch | Lazy, zero-copy |
| `MemoryRecords.filterTo()` | O(n records) | Decompresses each compressed batch |
| `MemoryRecords.slice()` | O(1) | Zero-copy `ByteBuffer` duplicate |
| `DefaultRecordBatch.sizeInBytes(Iterable)` | O(n records) | Iterates all records to sum sizes |
| `DefaultRecordBatch.writeHeader()` | O(n) | CRC computed over batch payload |

---

## What Was Not Documented

Per the task requirements, the following were intentionally excluded:

- **Private methods and fields** in all three classes.
- **Package-private and inner classes** (e.g., `RecordIterator`, `DecompressedRecordIterator`
  inside `DefaultRecordBatch`; `FilterResult`, `RecordFilter` inner types in `MemoryRecords`).
- **Implementation logic**: no algorithmic changes were made.

Running command: python3 - <<'EOF'
import re

files = {
    "RecordBatch.java": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
    "DefaultRecordBatch.java": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java",
    "MemoryRecords.java": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java",
}

print("=" * 70)
print("FINAL VERIFICATION REPORT")
print("=" * 70)

total_javadoc = 0
total_params = 0
total_returns = 0
total_throws = 0
total_sees = 0

for name, path in files.items():
    with open(path) as f:
        content = f.read()
    lines = content.splitlines()

    # Count Javadoc blocks
    javadoc_blocks = len(re.findall(r'/\*\*', content))
    # Count tags
    params  = len(re.findall(r'@param\s+\w+', content))
    returns = len(re.findall(r'@return\b', content))
    throws  = len(re.findall(r'@throws\b', content))
    sees    = len(re.findall(r'@see\b', content))

    # Verify no private method/field Javadoc (private members should not have /**)
    private_javadoc = []
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if re.search(r'\bprivate\b', line) and i > 0:
            prev = lines[i-1].strip()
            if prev == '*/':
                private_javadoc.append(i+1)

    # Verify class-level Javadoc exists (first /** before class declaration)
    class_match = re.search(r'/\*\*.*?\*/\s*public\s+(?:interface|class|abstract\s+class)', content, re.DOTALL)
    has_class_javadoc = bool(class_match)

    # Verify no implementation lines were accidentally removed
    # Check key sentinel constants still present
    sentinels_ok = True
    if name == "RecordBatch.java":
        for sentinel in ["NO_TIMESTAMP", "NO_SEQUENCE", "NO_PRODUCER_ID", "RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD"]:
            if sentinel not in content:
                sentinels_ok = False
                print(f"  MISSING SENTINEL: {sentinel}")
    if name == "DefaultRecordBatch.java":
        for impl in ["buffer.putLong", "ByteUtils.writeUnsignedInt", "incrementSequence", "decrementSequence"]:
            if impl not in content:
                sentinels_ok = False
                print(f"  MISSING IMPL: {impl}")
    if name == "MemoryRecords.java":
        for impl in ["Utils.tryWriteTo", "buffer.limit()", "validBytes", "filterTo"]:
            if impl not in content:
                sentinels_ok = False
                print(f"  MISSING IMPL: {impl}")

    total_javadoc += javadoc_blocks
    total_params  += params
    total_returns += returns
    total_throws  += throws
    total_sees    += sees

    print(f"\n{name}")
    print(f"  Lines            : {len(lines)}")
    print(f"  Javadoc blocks   : {javadoc_blocks}")
    print(f"  @param tags      : {params}")
    print(f"  @return tags     : {returns}")
    print(f"  @throws tags     : {throws}")
    print(f"  @see tags        : {sees}")
    print(f"  Class Javadoc    : {'YES' if has_class_javadoc else 'MISSING'}")
    print(f"  Private Javadoc  : {'NONE (correct)' if not private_javadoc else f'WARNING lines {private_javadoc}'}")
    print(f"  Impl intact      : {'YES' if sentinels_ok else 'CHECK ABOVE'}")

print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("TOTALS")
print(f"  Javadoc blocks : {total_javadoc}")
print(f"  @param tags    : {total_params}")
print(f"  @return tags   : {total_returns}")
print(f"  @throws tags   : {total_throws}")
print(f"  @see tags      : {total_sees}")
print("=" * 70)
EOF

- DefaultRecordBatch extends AbstractRecordBatch implements MutableRecordBatch
- MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords (implements Records)
- RecordBatch is the top-level interface (extends Iterable<Record>)
- Magic v2 batch header: BaseOffset(8), Length(4), PartitionLeaderEpoch(4), Magic(1), CRC(4), Attributes(2), LastOffsetDelta(4), BaseTimestamp(8), MaxTimestamp(8), ProducerId(8), ProducerEpoch(2), BaseSequence(4), RecordsCount(4), Records[...]
- RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD = 61 bytes
- CRC-32C covers from Attributes to end of batch
- Attributes bits: 15-7 unused, 6=delete horizon, 5=control, 4=transactional, 3=timestamp type, 2-0=compression
- MemoryRecords.EMPTY is a singleton empty instance
- MemoryRecords has filter() for log compaction, builder() factory methods, withRecords/withIdempotentRecords/withTransactionalRecords convenience factories

## What each file received

### `RecordBatch.java` (interface)
- **Class-level Javadoc**: magic version history (v0→v1→v2), full 61-byte wire-format layout, CRC-32C coverage range, attributes bit layout (bits 0–6), compaction invariants, thread-safety guarantee, performance characteristics
- **All interface methods**: `baseOffset`, `lastOffset`, `nextOffset`, `magic`, `checksum`, `isValid`, `ensureValid`, `maxTimestamp`, `offsetOfMaxTimestamp`, `timestampType`, `producerId`, `producerEpoch`, `baseSequence`, `lastSequence`, `hasProducerId`, `isTransactional`, `isControlBatch`, `deleteHorizonMs`, `partitionLeaderEpoch`, `countOrNull`, `isCompressed`, `compressionType`, `sizeInBytes`, `writeTo`, `streamingIterator`, `iterator`
- **All constants**: `MAGIC_VALUE_V0/V1/V2`, `CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE`, `NO_TIMESTAMP`, `NO_SEQUENCE`, `NO_PRODUCER_ID`, `NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH`, `NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH`, `RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD`

### `DefaultRecordBatch.java` (concrete MutableRecordBatch)
- **Class-level Javadoc**: byte-offset schema for all 13 header fields, CRC-32C computation details, attributes bit layout, timestamp/delete-horizon duality, compaction invariants, thread-safety, performance
- **Public methods**: `baseTimestamp`, `recordInputStream`, `iterator`, `skipKeyValueIterator`, `setLastOffset`, `setMaxTimestamp`, `setPartitionLeaderEpoch`, `writeEmptyHeader` (static), `writeHeader` (static, 17-param), `sizeInBytes(long, Iterable<Record>)`, `sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord>)`, `incrementSequence`, `decrementSequence`

### `MemoryRecords.java` (concrete Records)
- **Class-level Javadoc**: two construction paths, filtering/compaction model, zero-copy slicing, thread-safety, performance
- **Instance methods**: `sizeInBytes`, `writeTo`, `writeFullyTo`, `validBytes`, `firstBatchSize`, `filterTo`, `buffer`, `slice`, `batches`
- **Static factories**: `readableRecords`; all 7 `builder(…)` overloads; all 6 `withRecords`, 3 `withIdempotentRecords`, 3 `withTransactionalRecords`, 3 `withEndTransactionMarker` overloads; `writeEndTransactionalMarker`; `withLeaderChangeMessage`, `withSnapshotHeaderRecord`, `withSnapshotFooterRecord`, `withVotersRecord`, `withKRaftVersionRecord`

---
